metadata.title:
SAYS INDIAN MAIDS WILL


SOLVE SERVANT PROBLEM

pages.raw_text:
special Correspondence 10 The Press


SEATTLE. Sept. 9.--The servant
problem has been solved at last!


So, at least, says Mrs. Thomas
Burke Of Seattle, generally regard
ed as the best dressed woman in
the exposition city.


But how? Simple enough.


Get an Indian maid-or a bevy
of them, II your establishment war
rants. The Indian is the new do
nestle servant.


Mrs. Burke knows, for she EN-.
ploys descendants OF the original
owners of America in her house.
hold. Her cook is an Objibwa girl,
and her chauffeur has Chippewa
blood in his veins.


These facts came out the other
day at meeting of Indian edu-
Gators at the Alaska Yukon expo
sition, which was secured by the
efforts Of Miss Estelle Reel, super
intendent of the government IN
dian schools. Miss Reel gathered
at Seattle pupils of many Indian
schools and by practical demon
stration showed what progress the
aborigine is making under the in
vader's tutilage.


Then Mrs. Burke, whose hus-
band, Judge Thomas Burke, is one
of the richest men in the north.
west. formerly James J. Hills right
hand man on the coast, came to the
front and let her cook demonstrate
her professional ability. The DEM
onstration was a great success.
Sadie Quake" as Mrs. Burke's
servant is named, made good.


At this same meeting grand
daughter Of Chief Seattle, whose
name was taken by the northwes.
metropolis, demonstrated the value
Of her domestic science and train
ing by cooking most edible repast
for Mrs. Richard A. Ballinger, wife
of the secretary of the interior.


Eyes, indeed, I believe the In
dian can solve our domestic prob
lens and solve them right" said
Mrs. Burke. II am doing all l can
to introduce the dusky maids into
my friends homes and many have
